name: Las Vegas (United States)

colors.symbolism: the marquees of the city seal — tourism

description: A royal-blue field with a single broad grey diagonal stripe running from the upper hoist to the lower fly; the official seal of the City of Las Vegas is set on the canton, breaking the stripe.
history: The Las Vegas City Council authorised a contest for a city flag on 7 April 1965; the winning design — by Kenneth A. Bouton, an assistant to the city manager — was officially adopted on 2 October 1968. On the same day blue and silver were adopted as the city's official colours. The seal placed on the flag was designed by Richard Thompson and adopted on 16 March 1966.

features.symbolism: royal-blue field, representing the blue skies of Nevada
a broad grey diagonal stripe alluding to Nevada's nickname "The Silver State"

features.role: seal of the City of Las Vegas (1966)
features.symbolism: the city seal at the upper hoist, breaking the silver stripe; depicts three tall buildings of varying heights with yellow marquees stepping down from left to right, symbolising Las Vegas's tourism and entertainment industry
